title: ericameria gilmanii
intro: Ericameria gilmanii, also known as Gilman's rabbitbrush, is a shrub-like plant native to the southwestern United States. It is a member of the daisy family and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and digestive problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Ericameria gilmanii can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
